name: Fulgurite
primary_sources: The Voyage of the Beagle by Charles Darwin
Yale University Peabody Museum of Natural History

distinctive_features: Smooth interior with rough or porous exterior
visual_attributes.texture: Glassy, smooth interior with rough or porous exterior
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: Can exceed 20 cm in diameter and penetrate up to 15 m deep
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: Branching network with central axial voids

domain_specific_visual.SILICA_RICH: Often composed of silica, forming lechatelierite

domain_specific_functional.CLIMATE_INDICATOR: Used to estimate historical lightning frequency
domain_specific_functional.EXTREME_ENVIRONMENT_MINERALS: Contains minerals formed in extreme conditions

relationships.description: Fulgurites are formed when lightning strikes the ground

contextual_attributes.origin: Formed globally wherever lightning strikes suitable substrates
contextual_attributes.historical_period: Observed since ancient times, noted by Avicenna and Al-Biruni
contextual_attributes.geographic_range: Found worldwide, notable examples in Florida and Michigan
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Misinterpreted historically, now understood as natural phenomena
primary_sources: The Voyage of the Beagle by Charles Darwin
